back_translation: And the Philistines were fighting against Israel, and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and the slain fell on Mount Gilboa.
And the Philistines pursued closely after Saul and his sons, and the Philistines struck down Jonathan and Abinadab and Malchishua, sons of Saul.
And the battle was heavy against Saul, and the archers found him, men with the bow, and he was greatly wounded by the archers.

And Saul said to his armor-bearer, 'Draw your sword and pierce me through with it, lest these uncircumcised come and pierce me through and make sport of me.' But his armor-bearer was unwilling, for he was very afraid; so Saul took the sword and fell on it.
And his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, and he also fell on his sword and died with him.
And Saul died, and his three sons, and his armor-bearer, also all his men, on that day together.

And the men of Israel who were across the valley and across the Jordan saw that the men of Israel had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead; and they abandoned the cities and fled, and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.
And it happened on the next day, that the Philistines came to strip the slain, and they found Saul and his three sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.
And they cut off his head and stripped off his armor, and sent it throughout the land of the Philistines around, to proclaim it in the house of their idols and to the people.
And they put his armor in the house of Ashtaroth, and his body they fastened to the wall of Beth-shan.
And the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead heard concerning him what the Philistines had done to Saul.
And all the valiant men arose and went all night, and took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons from the wall of Beth-shan, and came to Jabesh and burned them there.
And they took their bones and buried them under the tamarisk tree at Jabesh, and they fasted seven days.
